summary: Salesforce — sync error retry queue with dead-letter handling
description_md:
As a workspace-admin, I want the Salesforce connector to automatically retry failed sync operations using a configurable retry queue, so that transient connectivity issues don't result in permanent data loss and I can review permanently failed records in a dead-letter queue for manual intervention.

## Background

The Salesforce connector currently fails silently when encountering transient API errors, causing data inconsistencies between the source and the platform. Implementing a retry queue with dead-letter handling will improve data reliability and provide visibility into persistent sync failures.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. Configure retry policy with exponential backoff (max 5 attempts, 30-minute window) for transient Salesforce API errors (HTTP 429, 503).
2. Move records that fail all retry attempts to a dead-letter queue with full error context including last error code, timestamp, and failed payload.
3. Provide workspace-admin UI to view, filter, and manually reprocess dead-letter queue entries with success/failure feedback.
4. Emit audit log entries for each retry attempt and dead-letter move, including record ID, attempt number, error details, and final disposition.

comments.author: dev2@amisol-demo.example
comments.body_md: Implementation complete. Retry queue uses Redis-backed storage with configurable TTL. Dead-letter entries persist indefinitely until manually reprocessed or purged by workspace-admin.
